html:
Frederick VI of Baden-Durlach, born on November 16, 1617, at Karlsburg Castle, and died there on January 10, 1677, was the Margrave of Baden-Durlach from 1659.

== Biography == Frederick VI was the son of Frederick V of Baden-Durlach and Barbara of Württemberg. Frederick VI participated with the Weimar, Hessian, and Swedish troops in the final stages of the Thirty Years' War. In 1642, he married Christina Magdalena of Palatinate-Zweibrücken, sister of Charles X Gustav. In 1655, this monarch appointed Frederick VI as a general in the Swedish cavalry. In this role, he participated in the war against Poland but returned home upon his father's death in 1659 and assumed the margraviate. Later, as an Imperial Field Marshal, Frederick VI participated in the war against France from 1674 to 1676. Children (selection):

Frederick VII of Baden-Durlach

How certain are we about this?

Figures from the era of great powers are extensively documented in archives and contemporary publications. Most information about who they were and what they did is well-documented; uncertainty lies in interpretation and motives.
tldr: Frederick VI was a German nobleman and military leader who lived in the 17th century. He held the titles of Margrave and General within the Swedish army.
lattlast:
Frederick VI was born in 1617 and died in 1677. He was an important figure within the nobility and became the Margrave of Baden-Durlach when his father died in 1659.

He was also a high-ranking military leader. He was appointed General in the Swedish army and participated in several major wars during the 17th century, including conflicts against Poland and France.

Frederick married Christina Magdalena, who was the sister of the Swedish King Charles X Gustav. He had children and is today a historically documented figure from the era of great power politics.
